title: galeobdolon luteum var. montanum
intro: Galeobdolon luteum var. montanum, also known as yellow archangel, is a perennial plant in the family Boraginaceae, native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of galeobdolon luteum var. montanum include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical application.
